provenance.source_corpus: Lichen matted seedbeds inhibit while moss dominated seedbeds facilitate black spruce (Picea mariana) seedling regeneration in post-fire boreal forest

source_spans.text: Lichen matted seedbeds inhibit while moss dominated seedbeds facilitate black spruce (Picea mariana) seedling regeneration
Lichen matted seedbeds inhibit while moss dominated seedbeds facilitate black spruce (Picea mariana) seedling regeneration
Lichen matted seedbeds inhibit while moss dominated seedbeds facilitate black spruce (Picea mariana) seedling regeneration
Black spruce germination and seedling growth was lowest in presence of Cladonia cristatella
Black spruce germination and seedling growth was lowest in presence of Cladonia cristatella
Seedling establishment was higher in mat mixed plots in the late post-ﬁres site (TNR)
Black spruce germination and seedling growth was lowest in presence of Cladonia cristatella
HPLC analysis of C. cristatella indicated the presence of usnic acid, a common germination inhibiting allelochemical
Mat manipulation treatments (mat mixing and mat removal) eliminated the favourable microsite condition
seedling establishment was higher in mat mixed plots in the late post-ﬁres site (TNR)
black spruce germination and seedling establishment negatively related to mat thickness
Lichen seedbeds had lower moisture but higher surface temperature than moss seedbeds

Mat mixing may enhance seedling damage due to frost heaving and animal browsing
